title: Loosening the Held Breath
intention: To release the tension held in the body and let the breath soften and open.

lines.text: Welcome. Take a moment to arrive, just as you are.
Let your eyes close, or rest your gaze gently downward.
Settle into whatever is holding you right now.
There's nothing to fix here. Nowhere else to be.
Let the weight of your body be received by the surface beneath you.
Notice the air moving in, and the air moving out.
You don't need to change it yet. Just watch a few breaths pass.
Now, when you're ready, draw in a slow breath through the nose.
And let it go through the mouth, with a soft, audible sigh.
Again. Breathe in.
And sigh it all the way out.
Notice how the sigh lets something put itself down.
Bring your attention now to your shoulders.
Feel where they may be lifted, braced, holding on.
On your next out-breath, let them drop. Just a little.
And again on the next exhale, a touch lower.
Let the space between your shoulders and your ears grow long.
Now soften the jaw. Let the teeth part slightly.
Let the tongue rest, and the muscles of the face go quiet.
Move your attention down through the chest.
If it feels tight, breathe gently into that tightness.
You're not forcing it open. You're just keeping it company.
Let the belly be soft. Let it rise and fall on its own.
If a thought arrives, simply note it.
Let it pass, like a cloud, and return to the breath.
Take one more long sigh out, and let the whole body settle.
Feel how much lighter the breath has become.
There is nothing left to hold. The body knows how to rest.
Stay here a few moments, breathing easy.
Let each exhale loosen you a little more.
When you're ready, let your breathing find its natural rhythm.
Notice the quiet you've made room for.
Carry a little of this ease with you, gently, into what comes next.
And whenever you're ready, you can open your eyes. Rest well.
